content.problemStatement:
You're given two positive integers LLL and RRR (L<RL \lt RL<R).

Let N=R−L+1N = R-L+1N=R−L+1.

Consider the array A=[L,L+1,L+2,…,R]A = [L, L+1, L+2, \ldots, R]A=[L,L+1,L+2,…,R] of length NNN that contains every integer from LLL to RRR exactly once, in order.

Find any array BBB of length NNN such that:


Every integer from LLL to RRR appears exactly once in BBB; and
For each 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N, gcd⁡(Ai,Bi)=1\gcd(A_i, B_i) = 1gcd(Ai​,Bi​)=1.
If no such BBB exists, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line, containing two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.


Output Format

For each test case,

If no valid array BBB exists, print the single integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, print NNN space-separated integers denoting the array BBB.

Each integer from LLL to RRR should appear exactly once, and gcd⁡(Ai,Bi)=1\gcd(A_i, B_i) = 1gcd(Ai​,Bi​)=1 should hold for each iii.



If there are multiple solutions possible, you may print **any of them**.
constraints: The sum of R−LR-LR−L across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: With L=3L = 3L=3 and R=7R = 7R=7, we have A=[3,4,5,6,7]A = [3, 4, 5, 6, 7]A=[3,4,5,6,7].
Consider B=[7,3,6,5,4]B = [7, 3, 6, 5, 4]B=[7,3,6,5,4]. Looking at each index:

gcd⁡(A1,B1)=gcd⁡(3,7)=1\gcd(A_1, B_1) = \gcd(3, 7) = 1gcd(A1​,B1​)=gcd(3,7)=1
gcd⁡(A2,B2)=gcd⁡(4,3)=1\gcd(A_2, B_2) = \gcd(4, 3) = 1gcd(A2​,B2​)=gcd(4,3)=1
gcd⁡(A3,B3)=gcd⁡(5,6)=1\gcd(A_3, B_3) = \gcd(5, 6) = 1gcd(A3​,B3​)=gcd(5,6)=1
gcd⁡(A4,B4)=gcd⁡(6,5)=1\gcd(A_4, B_4) = \gcd(6, 5) = 1gcd(A4​,B4​)=gcd(6,5)=1
gcd⁡(A5,B5)=gcd⁡(7,4)=1\gcd(A_5, B_5) = \gcd(7, 4) = 1gcd(A5​,B5​)=gcd(7,4)=1

BBB also contains every integer from LLL to RRR exactly once, so it's a valid array.
Test case 222: With L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3, we have A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3].
You may verify that the valid arrays BBB are [1,3,2],[2,3,1],[3,1,2][1, 3, 2], [2, 3, 1], [3, 1, 2][1,3,2],[2,3,1],[3,1,2].
Printing any one of them will be considered correct.
